tasks.title: New Video
tasks.prompt:
Interior. The apartment from Pulp Fiction where Vincent and Jules retrieve the briefcase. The lighting is grimy, 1990s Los Angeles morning. Jules stands near the young men on the couch, his voice rising with controlled fury. Vincent Vega leans against the kitchen counter in the background, watching silently with his coffee. Vinny Barbarino — dressed in his Welcome Back, Kotter outfit, hair perfect — stands in the center of the room, repeatedly saying “What?” in confusion, echoing Jules’s interrogation rhythm. Jules glares at him, jaw tight.
Jules yells: “Say what again!”
Vinny tilts his head, innocently: “What?”
Freeze-frame on the tense absurdity — the crossover of two Travoltas in one cinematic universe. Camera pans between Vinny’s clueless face and Vincent’s disbelief in the kitchen. Hyperreal lighting, cinematic realism, grain texture of 35mm film, Tarantino framing, natural dialogue cadence.

tasks.title: New Video
tasks.prompt: Interior. A gritty Los Angeles apartment, 1990s aesthetic. Two suited men interrogate a terrified suspect. In the background stands a man with slick dark hair, mid-20s, confident but detached. Nearby, another version of him—same face, older, heavier, late 40s—watches from the doorway, cigarette in hand. The younger one speaks in a fast, rhythmic accent, repeating “What?” The older one looks uneasy, aware he’s seeing his past self. The lead interrogator finally snaps: “Say what again!” The moment freezes. Camera pans between the two identical men, a lifetime apart. 35 mm film texture, hyperreal lighting, Tarantino-style framing.

tasks.title: New Video
tasks.prompt: Happy Thanksgiving from the Sora team! This video is only visible to you until you choose to share it. Here’s the idea we used to create it: @anthony.padavano is upset that he’s stuck at the kids’ table on Thanksgiving.
